meaning: adj.鑳芥彁鍒版洿楂樹竴绾ф爣鍑嗙殑(=upgradeable)
examples: The idea of an upgradable machine was a big selling point back then.
Application software and File System are upgradable over the Internet.
Resume with 12 memory. Firmware upgradable. 64 minute buffer. Rechargeable batteries.
Top all that off with a lower starting price, and being user upgradable, and you have our home automation gears working in overdrive.
Since the MAINGEAR Solo is completely upgradable, it features support for a wide range of processor and also supports 22nm next generation processors.
